solution: 
```cpp
#include <algorithm>

class Solution {
public:
    bool canMeasureWater(int x, int y, int target) {
        if (target == 0) return true;
        if (target > x + y) return false;
        if (target == x || target == y) return true;
        
        int a = x;
        int b = y;
        while (b != 0) {
            int temp = b;
            b = a % b;
            a = temp;
        }
        int gcd_val = a;
        return (target % gcd_val) == 0;
    }
};
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log(min(x, y)))
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses the mathematical property that the target must be a multiple of the greatest common divisor (GCD) of the two jug sizes and must not exceed their combined capacity. This approach efficiently checks the necessary conditions using the Euclidean algorithm to compute the GCD, ensuring optimal performance.
